entries.why: Private class used to enumerate, launch, or install other apps. App Review treats any use as non-public API.
entries.alternative: Use public LaunchServices, UIApplication.open(_:), or SKStoreProductViewController.

entries.why: Reads device identifiers and capabilities from MobileGestalt — a classic fingerprinting / private-API rejection.
entries.alternative: Use UIDevice, DeviceCheck, or identifierForVendor for the data you actually need.

entries.why: Boolean MobileGestalt query for private device capability flags.
entries.alternative: Check capabilities through public APIs (e.g. UIDevice, AVFoundation) instead.

entries.why: Launches other apps via SpringBoard — private SPI, not available to third-party apps.
entries.alternative: Use UIApplication.open(_:options:) with a public URL scheme or universal link.

entries.why: Enumerates installed apps — privacy-sensitive private SPI.
entries.alternative: There is no public equivalent; reconsider the feature or use canOpenURL for specific schemes.

entries.why: Direct telephony server connection — private SPI used to read call/cell state Apple does not expose.
entries.alternative: Use the public CoreTelephony APIs (CTCarrier, CTCallCenter) for what's allowed.

entries.why: Low-level event injection/observation — private and frequently abused for synthetic input.
entries.alternative: Use UIKit gesture recognizers and the responder chain.

entries.why: Controls display/backlight state through private SPI.
entries.alternative: Use UIScreen.brightness and isIdleTimerDisabled for permitted control.

entries.why: Private system service used to launch apps and observe system state.
entries.alternative: No public equivalent; rethink the interaction within the app sandbox.

entries.why: Touches private push-notification environment internals.
entries.alternative: Configure push via the aps-environment entitlement and UNUserNotificationCenter.

entries.why: Force-sets device orientation via a private setter — a long-standing rejection trigger.
entries.alternative: Use supportedInterfaceOrientations and setNeedsUpdateOfSupportedInterfaceOrientations().

entries.why: Private hook to simulate memory pressure; not allowed in shipping apps.
entries.alternative: Respond to didReceiveMemoryWarning; test pressure with Instruments, not in-app.

entries.why: Private debugging method. Harmless in intent but its symbol triggers automated non-public-API scans.
entries.alternative: Use it only in the debugger (po view.recursiveDescription); strip from shipping code.

entries.why: Private Auto Layout debugging method; flagged by static scans even though it's debug-only.
entries.alternative: Keep it to debugger sessions; don't reference it in compiled code.

entries.why: Selector on the private app-listing class; enumerates installed apps.
entries.alternative: No public equivalent; use canOpenURL for specific known schemes.

entries.why: Returns every installed app — privacy-sensitive private SPI.
entries.alternative: No public equivalent; reconsider the feature.

entries.why: Reads detailed battery internals via private IOKit power management.
entries.alternative: Use UIDevice.batteryLevel / batteryState.

entries.why: Obtains the SpringBoard Mach port — direct private system access.
entries.alternative: No public equivalent within the app sandbox.

entries.why: Private keyboard implementation class; reaching into it bypasses public text input.
entries.alternative: Use UITextInput, UIKeyInput, and the public keyboard notifications.

entries.why: Private web view internals behind the old UIWebView.
entries.alternative: Use WKWebView.

entries.why: Private memory/resource introspection used by Apple's own tooling.
entries.alternative: Use os_proc_available_memory() and MetricKit for memory insight.

entries.why: Direct access to the push daemon connection — private SPI.
entries.alternative: Use UNUserNotificationCenter and the standard remote-notification flow.

entries.why: Private text-input preferences (autocorrect, keyboard) controller.
entries.alternative: Use public UITextInputTraits configuration.

entries.why: Private badge setter that bypasses the notification system.
entries.alternative: Use UNUserNotificationCenter setBadgeCount / applicationIconBadgeNumber.
